metadata.title: fluxLab.dev — Software Development Studio | Kyiv, Ukraine
metadata.description: Kyiv-based dev studio building production SaaS products with React, Next.js, TypeScript & Go. 6 live products, 46K+ users. Custom web & mobile development.

hero.title: 6 Products. 46K+ Users. One Team.
hero.subtitle: Kyiv-based dev studio that builds, ships, and scales SaaS products. Need the same team behind your next project?

whyUs.title: Why fluxLab?
whyUs.subtitle: What makes us different from other dev studios
whyUs.productBuildersDesc: We've built 6 of our own SaaS products. We think in user flows and business metrics, not just tickets.
whyUs.fullOwnershipDesc: One team from design to deployment. No hand-offs, no gaps.
whyUs.battleTestedDesc: React, Next.js, Node.js, PostgreSQL — same stack powering our 46K+ user products.
whyUs.transparentDesc: Weekly demos, shared repos, direct Slack access. You see everything.

projects.title: Our Projects
projects.subtitle: Explore our portfolio of digital products
projects.getStartedDesc: Let's discuss how we can help you solve your business challenges with custom software.

0.title: Application Tracking — List View
0.description: The central hub for managing your job search pipeline. Every application is displayed as a card showing the job title, company, role, current status, linked resume, and timestamps.
features: Color-coded status badges: Active, Offer, Rejected, On Hold, Archived
Sort by Last Activity, Status, or Applied Date
Toggle between List and Board views
Each card shows linked resume and application/update dates

1.title: Application Tracking — Kanban Board
1.description: Visualize your pipeline as a Kanban board organized by status or interview stage. Drag-and-drop cards between columns to update application progress instantly.
features: Two board modes: By Status and By Stage
Drag-and-drop with optimistic UI updates
Column counts show pipeline distribution at a glance
Same card details as list view — no context lost

2.title: Resume Management & AI Builder
2.description: Manage multiple resume versions — both uploaded PDFs and resumes built directly in Jobber. The AI builder generates professional summaries and bullet points tailored to your experience.
features: Filter by All, Uploaded, or Built resumes
Visual resume previews with live formatting
Sort by Last Modified, Created Date, or Title
Track which resumes are used in how many applications
Active/Inactive status to manage resume versions

3.title: Company Management
3.description: Organize and track all companies you're applying to. Each card shows enriched statistics calculated from your applications — total count, active applications, last activity, and derived status.
features: Auto-derived company status: Idle, Active, Interviewing
Total and active application counts per company
Sort by Name, Last Activity, or Applications count
Quick "View Applications" link to filtered application list
Company location and notes for context

4.title: Job Board & Chrome Extension Import
4.description: Browse and manage all saved job postings. Jobs can be added manually or imported from any website with one click using the Chrome extension — AI automatically parses job details.
features: Chrome extension banner: save jobs from any website
Sort by Created Date, Job Title, or Company Name
Each card shows company, source (LinkedIn, Referral, etc.), posting link, and application count
View Posting links open the original job listing
AI-powered parsing extracts title, company, location, and salary

5.title: AI Cover Letter Generation
5.description: Create and manage cover letters tailored to specific jobs and companies. AI generates drafts based on the job description and your resume, which you can edit and refine.
features: Card-based gallery of all cover letters
Each letter linked to a specific company
AI generation with one-click improvements
Edit timestamps to track latest versions
Create new cover letters with the "+" button

6.title: Customizable Interview Stage Templates
6.description: Define your interview pipeline stages once, then reuse them across all applications. Recommended stages can be added with one click, or create custom stages that match your process.
features: Recommended stages: Applied, Phone Screen, Technical Interview, On-site, HR, Offer, Rejected
One-click "Add All" to apply recommended stages
Custom stage creation with ordering
Edit and delete stages with confirmation
Numbered sequence for clear pipeline visualization

7.title: Analytics Dashboard
7.description: Get data-driven insights into your job search. The dashboard shows KPI cards, a visual application funnel with conversion rates, and additional reports for time-in-stage, resume effectiveness, and source performance.
features: KPI cards: Total Applications, Active, Closed, Response Rate, Avg. Response Time
Application Funnel: visual stage-by-stage conversion with drop-off rates
Time in Stages: average days spent at each interview step
Resume Effectiveness: compare which resume versions perform best
Application Sources: track which job boards produce the most results

engagement.title: Engagement Models
engagement.subtitle: Flexible options to fit your needs
engagement.dedicatedTeamDesc: 2-5 developers embedded in your workflow. Ideal for long-term product development and ongoing feature work.
engagement.projectBasedDesc: Fixed scope, clear milestones, predictable budget. Best for well-defined products with a known deadline.
engagement.staffAugDesc: Individual senior developers who plug into your existing team. Perfect for filling skill gaps fast.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.a1: Most projects take 4-12 weeks from kickoff to launch, depending on scope. We define milestones upfront so you always know where things stand.
faq.a2: Shared Slack channel, weekly demo calls, and access to our project management board. You're never in the dark.
faq.a3: Yes — our staff augmentation model lets you bring on senior developers one at a time, fully integrated into your team.
faq.a4: We offer ongoing support and maintenance packages. Many clients keep us on retainer for feature development and bug fixes.
faq.a5: Absolutely. We offer flexible engagement models that scale with your budget. Several of our own products started as lean MVPs.

blog.title: Blog
blog.subtitle: Insights, tutorials, and updates from our team

mission.title: Our Mission
mission.description: We started as four developers who met on a project and discovered we build better together. After selling our first product, we went all-in on creating software that solves real problems. Today, fluxLab.dev is a product studio with six applications and a shared belief that great code changes lives.

values.title: Our Values
values.qualityDesc: We never compromise on code quality and user experience
values.innovationDesc: We embrace new technologies and creative solutions
values.transparencyDesc: Open communication and honest relationships with clients
values.growthDesc: We invest in learning and professional development

team.title: Our Team
team.subtitle: Meet the people behind the products

0.bio: Full-stack developer with 8+ years of experience building scalable web applications. Architected all six fluxLab products from the ground up.

1.bio: React specialist focused on building beautiful, accessible user interfaces. Leads the design system powering all fluxLab products.

2.bio: Backend engineer specializing in Node.js, databases, and cloud infrastructure. Built the API layer handling 890K+ monthly transactions.

3.bio: Designer crafting intuitive and visually compelling digital experiences. Responsible for the UX across all six products in the portfolio.

2021.title: How We Met
2021.description: Our paths crossed on a large-scale enterprise project. Four developers from different backgrounds found themselves on the same team, and the chemistry was immediate. We shared the same drive for clean code, user-centric design, and late-night debugging sessions fueled by too much coffee.

2022.title: The Side Project
2022.description: When the corporate contract ended, we weren't ready to go our separate ways. We started meeting on weekends to build a SaaS tool we wished had existed during our day jobs. What began as a casual experiment quickly turned into something real — a polished product that people actually wanted to use.

2023.title: First Exit
2023.description: Our side project caught the attention of a larger company, and we closed our first acquisition deal. The experience was eye-opening: we realized we could build, ship, and sell software as a team. We quit our jobs and officially founded fluxLab.dev as a full-time studio.

2024.title: Building for the Market
2024.description: Instead of chasing client contracts, we made a bold decision: build our own products. We launched EFluxCom, WashFlow, and our Accounting platform — each solving real problems we had observed across industries. The bet paid off as all three gained traction with paying customers.

2025.title: Scaling the Portfolio
2025.description: With three successful products under our belt, we expanded to six. Jobber, Market, and LifeSpan joined the lineup. Our team grew, our processes matured, and our vision sharpened: create software that works so well, it sells itself. This is just the beginning.

footer.description: Software development studio building modern digital products.

efluxcom.quote: Reduced our energy monitoring costs by 27% in the first quarter. The dashboard gives us real-time visibility we never had before.

washflow.quote: Went from spreadsheets to managing 120 locations in one platform. Our operators finally have the tools they deserve.

accounting.quote: 890K+ invoices processed with zero downtime. The automation alone saved us 40 hours per week across the team.
